Description: Around 3,000 attendees at a sermon misheard the word "fight" as "fire" and tried to evacuate the building simultaneously in the ensuing chaos. Many people were killed when they fell over in the staircase outside the exit. Most of the fatalities were women.

A movie screening for children was held at a public hall. The supervising staff directed children to the lower floor when the upper floor became overcrowded. The crowd that attempted to go down the stairs were not able to, due to overcrowding on the lower floor. 16 children died and 17 were slighly injured.

A false shout of "fire" caused panic during a movie screening. A projector malfunction created a flash of light, which led theatergoers to rush to the exit. The narrow exit and locked doors caused a bottleneck, trapping people in the stairwell, and 26 people died. One individual who escaped later died from injuries.

Someone at a miners' Christmas party held on the second floor of an assembly hall shouted "fire", and attendees rushed to escape down the stairs. The crowd was squeezed against the inward-opening doors at the bottom of the stairs. Of the 73 people who died, 59 of them were children.

An accident occurred during an air raid in World War I in Paris. As residents sought refuge in the Bolivar station, panic ensued when the station's doors, which only opened outward, blocked their escape. The crowd became trapped, leading to suffocation and crushing deaths.

The projector began to emit smoke during a packed movie screening. Around 1,000 children tried to evacuate at once. Children piled up down the stairs, in front of the emergency exit  which was padlocked shut.

A crowd of around 10,000 people, far in excess of the station’s capacity, gathered to see off new recruits to the army. Before the train carrying the recruits was able to depart, pushing broke out on the stairs to the station overpass, causing members of the crowd to fall over. 77 people were killed and 74 injured.

In the day which represents the record attendance at Hillsborough stadium (72,841 people) a men was pressed against a gate when trying to get into the already closed stadium (some suggest the locked gate collapsed due to crowd pressure).
Hundreds of people climbed the roof of an already capacity filled stadium despite the police asking them to refrain doing so. The roof collapsed over the spectators killing 2 and injuring another 15.

The day before a large air raid had dropped tons of bombs, so, at the day of the accident, when the raid alarm rang, people rushed into a tunnel shelter and some fell from the stairs while people outside kept trying to get in.
A woman carrying a child fell over on the stairs while a large crowd tried to enter the station to use it as an air raid shelter during an air raid, resulting in many people getting killed.
A fence in the stands at a crowded soccer game collapsed, and many in the crowd fell over and were killed. It is estimated that 85,000 attended the match.

Almost 6,000 people gained for a religious event and tried to leave simultaneously when someone yelled "fire". Many people tried to leave from a gate, unaware that is was closed. It is believed "fire" was said by some people having criminal intentions.

Several hundreds of people got killed in the crowd during Stalin's public funeral as citizens tried to pay their respects to Stalin's casket. Exact number is unknown with some believing being over thousand.

When a rope restricting pedestrian access to the bridge was lifted, the crowd surged forward into the newly available space. An old woman stumbled and fell, causing a chain reaction of people falling over each other.

A surge in the crowd at an important Hindu religious event (Kumbh Mela) and a destination for pilgrimages, caused a fence to collapse and a crowd accident. It is believed 5 million pigrims gained for the 40-days festival in which bathing plays an fundamental role.

Ticket sales for a highly expected game was set to take place at the stadium on the day of the match. Thousands of people started to pack days before the sale started and when gates suddenly opened people rushed in, eventually leading to a crowd collective motion. More than 150 people were injured.

Crowds attempting to leave an important religious ceremony via the third gate of the shrine ran into a crowd trying to gain entrance to the ceremony, resulting in a stairway accident. 77 people were seriously injured.

Crowd surged forward after a goal was scored eventually leading to the collapse of a wall whose bricks fell over children seating beneath. One boy was killed. Wall construction was judged as sound, but the absence of dedicated barriers contributed to pressure built-up.

During a public consolation event held at the Busan Public Stadium, a sudden rain shower caused approximately 30,000 spectators to rush toward the narrow entrance, which was only 7 to 8 meters wide, to seek shelter. In the chaos, people fell onto the dark, sloping road outside the stadium.

The tragedy took place when a crowd of 3,900 people gathered at the station's platform. The station, which usually handled 1,300 people a day, was overcrowded, and when an announcement was made, passengers rushed toward the stairs, causing several to slip and be crushed. The chaotic situation was worsened by the icy conditions.
A last minute-draw, when people already started leaving, resulted in a surge in an already crowded stairway as people tried to get back to join celebrations.

A surge occurred in an reportedly overfilled stadium (apperently more than 100'000 for a stadium accomodating 62'000) during a soccer game. A 15 year-old girl and a 45 year-old man died and many hundreds were injured.

An unpopular call by a referee resulted in a pitch invasion, and police fired tear gas in response. Scared spectators fled the stadium via the stairs leading outside. The exits were closed, and the resulting crowd pressure caused people to get killed.
A riot broke out between fans of opposite teams and the fleeing of people from violence resulted in an accident in front of the stand exit.

Extreme behavior by some spectators scared the rest of the crowd which tried to evacuate via the stairs leading outside. The exits were locked, and the resulting crowd pressure led to an accident. Approximately 150 people were injured.

Spectators attempting to leave the stadium at the end of a soccer game all tried to access the narrow stairs at once. A single person falling over caused a large number of other people to fall in a chain reaction. It is believed more than 80,000 fans attended the game.

On the reinauguration of the expanded stadium rumors about a possible collapse of the stadium resulted in a tumult as people rushed to the pitch. Several thousands were hurt in the stadium filled with over 100,000 spectators (some even reporting as much as 150,000).

Venue for the match had been changed to a stadium with a capacity lower then the one where the game was initially planned. Worried they will not be able to get in fans rushed to the new venue and a wall collapsed under the pressure of the crowd. At least 48 lost their lives and 50 were injured.
A crowd of 35,000 people at White City Stadium in West London was involved in a dangerous surge during a concert, causing 800 injuries as people rushed toward the front.

An accident occurred on stairs as people were leaving after a hockey match. Is believed fans rushed to get souvenirs, although other factors (such as lights getting off) appearently contributed to the accident (details are unclear). Attendance was reportedly below capacity. Actual number of fatalities is not clear.

A fire cracker lighted after a goal scared fans, who knocked down a soldier, who in turn accidentally killed two children with his gun. The confusion generated by the escalating situation resulted in several people getting killed in the moving crowd, although numbers are unclear. The soldier eventually commited suicide later on.

Some people started shooting during a political rally attended by 500,000. Intervening security forces entered the scene with sirens and explosives, further scaring the crowd already trying to flee. From autopsy results it is believed that 27 people died due to crush injuries (with others being killed by bullets).

An accident occurred during a soccer game when the floodlights were suddenly switched off. People started leaving the stadium unaware that some gates were locked and packed up in front of the barriers. 24 people were killed and dozens were injured.

An estimated number of 200,000 fans attended a concert in a stadium designed for 30,000. As a result the stadium collapsed, killing 9. It is reported that over 90,000 tickets were printed. All fatalities involved children, with dozens more injured.

Believing that the concert had started earlier than expected, a large number of people with first-come-first-served tickets flooded the entrance to the venue. People pushing to get to the entrance caused a rise in crowd pressure, resulting in a serious accident. 26 people were injured.

To avoid a possible clash between fans, exit was staged with home supporters leaving first. Pressure built up among visiting supporters standing in front of the gates resulted in the collapse of a brick wall.

During the bullfighting festival known as Las Fiestas de Corraleja, a tragic accident occurred when overcrowding led to the collapse of the wooden bleachers in the temporary bullring, killing at least 222 people.

People struggled to enter the venue as gates opened in front of a huge crowd which gathered to see an important religious leader. Half a milion people are said to had gathered to the event. 72 were injured, some of them critically.

Stadium gates' were forced open by a large crowd trying to enter the venue during the visit of an important religious leader. The crowds surged through the open gates where fatalities occurred. More than 30 people were injured.
People rushed to the exit after a confined electric fire developed in a cinema. Most of the victims were children. 45 people were injured.

People escaped from an escalating violence among fans of opposing team in a stadium without divisions between both groups of supporters. Spectactors perished at the exit gates trying to escape the riot. More than 100 were injured.

Fans rushed out of the stadium to celebrate after a game, but, as a result of some of the stadium exits being partially closed, the crowd density of leaving spectators increased, leading to an accident where 11 people died and more than 50 were injured.

A lighting failure on the staircase caused 300 to 400 visitors to rush to the exit in a famous tower visited by many tourists. The chaos resulted in 45 deaths, mostly school children, and several injuries. Following the incident, the tower was closed to the public, and stricter entry regulations were implemented.

Fans from the home team tried to go back to the stadium after a goal was scored 20 seconds before the end. Because of the low attendance only one exit was used and groups from both directions clashed on the partially frozen stairs. Dispute exist on the number of fatalities, some reporting up to 340 people.

People started to flow out of the stadium as result seemed certain. A quick draw led some to come back but were blocked by others escaping from rowdy fans who started urinating from the upper deck. An accident occurred at the exit where a railing collapsed. More than 100 were injured.
A packed roof collapsed after 20,000 people gathered in a stadium with a capacity for 15,000. 584 people were injured. Most of the deaths were children.

People died inside a tunnel as they tried to enter an already full stadium before the start of a well anticipated game. Reports speculate that false tickets were sold and point out weaknesses of doors which allowed people to force their entry.

A riot broke out between home fans and visiting supporters before the start of a soccer game. The rest of the crowd tried to flee, and those at the front were squeezed against a fence, resulting in 39 deaths.

Around 20,000 pilgrims had been waiting for two hours in a police cordon to cross a bridge during the Kumbh Mela. After the crowd surged forward the police responded with a mild baton charge creating confusion in the crowd and resulting in 47 fatalities and several injuries.

Rope barriers intended to control the large crowd of Hindu pilgrims during a religious procession in northern India collapsed, triggering an accident that left at least 32 people dead and 30 others injured.

At a religious festival involving about 8,000 participants (and 25,000 spectators), a man collapsed and died during the ritual. Shortly after midnight, inside the temple, participants were violently pushing and struggling to grab the sacred sticks. During this intense physical clash, a 39-year-old man became unresponsive and was carried out.
Twenty people died when terrified fans fled from knife-wielding attackers, causing a wall to collapse.

A thick fog led to the suspension of ferry service, leaving people waiting at the terminal for several hours. When the service was finally resumed and a ferry arrived, the crowd hurried to board, causing an accident. 70 were injured. It is estimated 30,000 to 40,000 had been waiting.

At a rap concert, around 1,000 youths crushed while trying to exit through a narrow, winding hallway, causing an accident that killed two teenage girls and injured at least 29 others. The chaos escalated when concertgoers began throwing cans of food, intended for charity, at police and firefighters.

Elementary school students leaving their classrooms for early morning assembly flooded a dark stairwell in which the lights had been switched off. A fall on the stairs resulted in the deaths of 28 people.

A sudden hailstorm at a soccer game caused an accident in the exit tunnel leading from the terrace as the spectators tried to evacuate. The stadium doors had been locked, preventing the crowd from escaping.
Around 50 people fell over about 13 m from the stage when the excited crowd at a rock concert surged forwards. Two people died.

A large number of spectators tried to enter the stands of a stadium at the start of a soccer match, despite the stands being surrounded on all sides by fences, which led to an incresase in density. 96 people died as a result of being pressed into the pitch-side fencing.

Hundreds of thousands of mourners gathered in Tehran to pay their respects to Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, the leader of Iran's Islamic revolution. During the event, a crowd surge caused by security losing control resulted in 35 deaths.

An incident occurred as a large number of pilgrims moved down a tunnel 550 m long and 10 m wide. Some people fell from a bridge onto pilgrims exiting the tunnel causing people to accumulate within it. That day outside temperature was 44 degrees Celsius.

An unpopular decision by the referee led to fights breaking out between spectators at a soccer match. The remaining spectators scared out and were pushed against riot control fences as they attempted to escape, resulting in the deaths of 40 people.
13,000 excited spectators surged towards the stage at a rock concert, leading to the deaths of 3 people.
A large number of pilgrims surged into the atrium of a church in order to receive blessings from God, leading to a crowd incident. 55 were injured.

A crowd crush occurred during a Zakat distribution at a cigarette factory with most details missing. The number of deaths were 32, out of which 27 persons died of Traumatic Asphyxia and 5 from head injury and more than 100 were injured, mostly elderly female and children. The young and elderly male escaped.
One  fan was killed and 24 others seriously injured during an Africa Cup of Nations qualifier match pitting Kenya against Mozambique. Details are unknown.

People moving against the flow of the crowd at a lantern festival in a park led to an incident on a bridge with poor lighting. Some of the 105 deaths were caused by people falling from the bridge itself.

A large number of spectators trying to enter an already crowded gym to watch a basketball game led to an incident in a small stairwell at the entrance. More tickets had been sold than the gym had capacity. There were 8 deaths and 29 injuries.

A crowd surge occurred during a religious event after the chief minister arrived for a holy dip. Reports are rather unclear and it appears that some deaths were related to a collapsed building but many also due to the pressure of the crowd. Two gates were used for about ten thousands pilgrims.

A temporary terrace collapsed killing 18 and injuring 2,300. The temporary facility was built to increase stadium capacity by 50% for an important match. The structure collapsed before the match started.

A grid collapsed leading to people falling 8 meters over people sitting beneath them. It is unclear what led people moving toward the grid, although it was found that structural failure was possibly caused by corrosion and lack in maintenance.

More than 15,000 drunken people gathered at the intersection of different red light districts for the New Year countdown. The high-density crowd that formed led to an incident. 21 people died and 67 were injured. The area in which they had gathered was on a steep slope.

Over 2,000 Muslims were evacuated from a besieged town in eastern Bosnia aboard United Nations trucks, but tragedy struck as six people were killed in the crush to escape. Upon arrival at a Bosnian Government-held town, the bodies of a woman and five children were found among the evacuees.

An incident occurred as thousands of people were trying to return to Canton after the Chinese New Year holidays. The accident was triggered when a man dropped his luggage on a pedestrian overpass, causing a pile-up of people as others behind him continued to push forward.
A crowd incident occurred amongst pilgrims attending the Stoning of the Devil ritual. Details on the accident remains mostly unclear.

An accident occurred at a church when a sudden storm caused a crowd of up to 50,000 people to rush for shelter. The event resulted in the deaths of over 140 people, mostly children aged 4 to 15, and many were injured.

During a show a fan was killed when the stadium gates were opened. The band was unaware of the incident and continued the performance. After learning of the death, they expressed their condolences through local networks to the victim's family and friends.

Security police baton-charged a crowd of about 40,000 people after order broke down at a demonstration. The incident occurred as the crowd tried to escape. At least 113 people died, primarily women and children. Fences and barbed wire barricades had been installed prior to the incident.

In the early hours of the morning, an accident occurred at an overcrowded nightclub, as young people fled a brawl and became trapped in a narrow stairwell. Four people, including a 13-year-old girl, were killed, and over 20 were injured as the crowd surged and bodies piled up.

Two people were killed and more than 150 people were injured in an accident at a rock concert. The incident occurred when the crowd of 20,000 people surged toward the concert doors. At least 20 people were seriously injured. Police and soldiers were called in to help control the crowd.

During a large religious gathering, an accident occurred, killing eight people and injuring 13 others. The meeting, attended by 30,000 worshipers, included claims of “miracle” cures. The cause of the stampede is unclear, with some reports suggesting that the appearance of a "serpent" caused the collective motion.

A deadly crush occurred at a concert at the front of the crowd, leading to the death of a 16-year-old girl and injuries to many others. Witnesses described being trapped in the crowd, with people losing their footing and others unable to get up as the mass surged and swayed. The band stopped playing and urged the crowd to calm down.

An accident occurred as people tried to flee the stadium and a wall collapsed. Reports are conflicting on what caused the sudden crowd motion, but the stadium was reported being well over capacity. At least 9 people died.

During a football match, riots erupted after spectators shouted anti-Gaddafi slogans. The situation escalated when bodyguards of Gaddafi's sons opened fire on the crowd, leading to a collective motion as thousands fled. Fans also stormed the pitch and attacked the referee, while rioters clashed with police and vandalized foreign cars.

Devotees trumbled in a narrow staircase inside a temple complex killing 39 people and injuring 35. Some were wounded by bamboo and steel wires as they were thrown against a temporary barricade. Gates' opening was delayed, appearently to let in VIP first.

Devotees got killed as they were crossing an overcrowded bridge used on both directions to take a holy dip, resulting in 21 fatalities and 40 people injured. It is said that more than 2 million people had gathered for the event.

Private security guards closed gates and attempted to control the crowd using electric cattle prods to prevent those without tickets to board a train after a campaign to crack down fare beaters was started.

An accident occurred before the game started in an reportedly overfilled stadium (possibly due to counterfeit tickets) leading to the collapse of a fence separating different areas. 147 people were injured.
Only two of the five gates were mistakanly opened at the end of a 40,000 packed soccer game. Crowds moved to the exits unaware that some of them were locked. It is believed stadium was over capacity.
In a concert attended by over 60,000 fans, a girl tragically died in the crowd. After discussions with security and police, the band chose to play softer songs to avoid further chaos.

At a concert over 100,000 fans gathered at a venue with a 40,000 capacity, resulting in a deadly accident. Two girls died from asphyxiation, and 107 others were injured. The overcrowding, poor event planning, and hysteria when the duo returned for an encore contributed to the disaster. Despite warnings from security, the performance continued.
Another incident among pilgrims attending the Stoning of the Devil ceremony killed 118 people and injured 180. Details are unclear.
A collective rush happened during celebrations for the independence day as thousands of spectators scrambled for seats for events which included a free soccer match between two popular teams.

An accident at a presidential office. The crowd, estimated at 10,000 to 15,000 people, had gathered outside to seek help for issues like jobs and housing. Chaos ensued when a group of men broke the line, causing a surge toward the gates. In the melee, two women were trampled to death, and 45 others were injured, some seriously.

A tear gas bomb was exploded inside a nightclub facility and 9 people were killed as they tried to escape outside. The only door available was closed by security personnel believing a gang wanted to enter to steal or start a fight. Only 30 or 40 of the 250 people present managed to leave the nightclub.

A landslide occurred at a temple during a religious pilgrimage, causing 200,000 male pilgrims to get scared and flee, which in turn caused a crowd incident. Some died in the landslide, but most were killed from the crowd itself. Most of the pilgrims were not familiar with the location as they came from far away.

A sudden thunderstorm during an outdoor concert caused many young attendees to attempt to take shelter in a nearby subway station. Some people slid and fell over on the wet sidewalk, causing a crowd incident.

As attendees left at the end of a snowboarding contest, many people slipped on the dark, steep slopes and fell into a barrier at the bottom of the hill. Dozens of people had been injured and were taken to the hospital, some in serious conditions.

A tear gas canister was trow inside a nightclub in the afternoon when children were filling the facility. The emergency door was locked and the only way out was down a narrow staircase leading to the main entrance. Fatalities are said to have occurred on the staircase, where a wall also collapsed.

An accident at a food distribution event resulted in the deaths of five people and left ten others injured. The incident occurred at a camp, which houses thousands of flood victims, when a large crowd rushed to receive aid. The chaos may have been triggered  a driver attempting to flee with a truck.
Two canisters of gas were set off in a popular nightclub at the same time when lights went out, leading to a blind escape. The nightclub recently passed safety regulations inspections.
A crowd surge occurred in an appearently overfilled stadium. One person died jumping from a fence to escape the dense crowd while others passed away for its consequences.

An accident occurred in a circus as guards used batons to break up an unruly crowd. It is believed people without ticket tried to force their entry in a break between shows when spectactors were leaving the circus. A special show was held that day close to where a religious congregation was also held.
An accident occurred in an amphitheatre during a memorial cerimony attended by thousands as children began running to cover from a rainstorm. More than 50 were injured, all victims were children.
Excited spectators surged towards the stage during a concert, resulting in 9 deaths and 26 injuries.

An accident occurred as police responded with tear gas to fans throwing plastic bottles in a near-capacity crowd causing many people to urge toward the exits. Fans leaving the stadium found more gas outside.
A teenage got killed in a mosh pit during a music festival. Barriers were not employed to split the crowd and were introduced in later editions following the incident.

An accident occurred as commuters rushed down stairs toward a main-line passenger train. Dynamics is not clear although it is believed security locked the gates and an annoucement on the sudden closure was made only 10 minutes before departure, leading passengers to rush.
An incident occurred among pilgrims attending the Stoning of the Devil ceremony.

Teenage girls got killed at the front of the stage during a performance of a boy band in a shopping mall. The band performed without a stage. The accident occurred as the band started signing autographs. A similar appearance was canceled after 20,000 stormed the venue.

Thousands of men crammed into a narrow street leading to the main door of a shrine. A collective rush occurred as the opening of the shrine was delayed by more than 3 hours with about 100,000 people waiting. Becoming restless amid the delay some of them opted to seek entry through a secondary gate out-of-bound leading to a scramble in which 44 got killed and about hundred were injured.

An accident at a temple fair killed 16 people and injured 6 others. The tragedy occurred when a large crowd became trapped in a narrow passageway leading to the temple. Despite efforts by police and local officials to manage the crowd and assist the injured, the incident resulted in fatalities.

A crowd of fans who were trying to find tickets for a soccer match surged into an already over-capacity stadium when the match began to heat up, destroying the perimeter fence in the process. The sudden flow of people caused a crowd incident near the entrance gate.

Police fired tear gas to unruly fans who had invaded the pitch leading to an ensuing crowd accident. Fans were believed to had thrown objects at each other. The main exit was blocked leading to no way out.
A roof collapsed as spectactors reportedly sat on top of a temporary scaffolding in a crammed stadium said to have been well above capacity. Hundreds have been injured.

Extreme behavior from some of the fans at a soccer match led to the police responding with tear gas. Many spectators tried to evacuate simultaneously. A crowd incident occurred because there were too few exits and the exits that did exist had been locked.

An incident occurred just before the end of a firework display, as a crowd of spectators trying to leave ran into a crowd of people going in the opposite direction on a pedestrian bridge between the railway station and the venue. 247 sustained injuries. The exit stairs of the pedestrian bridge acted as a bottleneck, as they were half the width of the bridge itself.

A worker in a garment factory sounded the alarm after seeing flames shooting from an electric circuit board. Workers converged to the stairs to find the single exit locked and the security guard absent.

An accident occurred at a festival when thousands of people tried to force their way into the venue, which was already over capacity. Police fired warning shots, triggering a surge that led to people being trampled. Witnesses offered conflicting accounts, some claiming a riot broke out, and others said the crowd was calm before the police intervention.

A crowd of about 60,000 people attended a gift distribution event. A wall collapsed and the resulting confusion killed 4 and left several injured. 10 militars were reportedly on-site to manage the large crowd, although 70 were planned to be on service.

A crowd incident occurred as attendees tried to enter the venue for a Christmas party because the stairs that led into the venue had become frozen and were slippery. Six people between the ages of 11 and 15 died.

During an evangelist crusade, three elderly men in their late 60s were feared dead in a crowd accident. The victims were reportedly trampled by the large crowd attending the miracle event on the second day of the crusade.

A series of explosions at a military depot triggered widespread panic, leading to the indirect deaths of hundreds people in crowd accidents. The massive blast killed around 300 people and sent debris across the city, igniting fires and causing chaos. Thousands of people fled toward a banana plantation, resulting in at least 600 drownings.

Gunmen attacked a weekly prayer meeting causing a collective flee that killed at least 12 people. The gunmen appeared to be targeting a priest, who was leading the meeting, but left without injuring anyone. The incident occurred as the open-air service was ending, and conflicting reports suggest the death toll may be higher.

Students in a middle school attempted to leave evening classes in complete darkness. Not being able to see, students could not understand the situation and kept pushing. Eventually a guardrail collapsed killing 21 and injuring 47. It is said two teacher were on duty to manage 1563 students and the school was designed for 800.

During a commotion people tried to protect others from becoming involved. As a result of this event the crowd was pushed down the stairs toward the two platforms, increasing crowd density at the end. In the chaos some climbed on an arriving train and got electified by the overhead high tension wire. A lot of people were arriving for a political rally.

A crowd accident killed at least 30 women and children and injured hundreds as people for free clothes being distributed by a local businessman. The stampede occurred outside an abandoned jute mill as the crowd surged forward when the gates were opened. The distribution attracted over 10,000 people, mostly poor women and children.

A power loss occurred in a middle school in the afternoon. As power could not be restored it was decided to cancel evening classes and let the students go home. Students in the classroom upstairs rushed down the stairways as students in the playground rushed upstairs to get their bags, eventually clashing on the stairs. 19 were injured.
An incident occurred among pilgrims attending the Stoning of the Devil ceremony.

Security guards trying to break up a fight at a club used pepper spray, causing chaos among the 1,500 attendees as many believed that they were being attacked by terrorists and tried to flee towards the exit. The exit was at the bottom of a steep flight of stairs and the door to the street was narrow, and opened inwards. 21 people died from suffocation.
An accident occurred as stadium gates were opened for a concert by a popular artist. Gates opening was delayed by a previous event held in the same venue.

The throwing of silver coins to a crowds of pilgrim leading to a religious event was believed to have caused a collective rush which killed 39 and injured about 150. A different source said the collective motion was caused as pilgrims rushed for the holy dip after sadhus (holy men), already late in their bathing, had finished.
An accident occurred on stairs after students were leaving a middle school in the darkness due to a power loss. 15 children were injured, 4 of them seriously.
An incident occurred among pilgrims attending the Stoning of the Devil ceremony. Over 200 people were injured.

A large number of people moving against the flow of the crowd caused an incident on a bridge between two venues that were separated by a river at a New Year lantern festival. 37 people died, many of them women and children. There were reports that the bridge was crowded with people watching fireworks at the time of the incident.
An accident at a rock concert killed four people and injured dozens more. The incident occurred as thousands of fans, mostly young, rushed to exit the soccer stadium after the performance.
An accident occurred as spectators escaped from rioting fans. More than 100 people were injured.

About 10,000 women with babies and small children attended a rally held in a park. The accident occurred as the end of the event was broadcasted and it was announted people could collect gifts at the reception counter. This led to a large number of women hurrying toward the reception counter near the exit. 21 women and children died.
The explosion of an electric transformer nearby initiated an evacuation in a six-story garment factory. Workers rushed on the stairwells killing 6 and injuring at least 30.

An overcrowded balcony collapsed in a nightclub, killing six people and injuring at least 117. The collapse occurred when the balcony, holding more people than its structure could support, gave way, crushing those below.

A furniture store held an event at which the first 50 people to attend would each receive a gift certificate worth 500 Rial, and the next 200 attendees would each receive 100 Rial vouchers. More than 8,000 people gathered outside the store before it opened. Despite being scheduled to open at 10am, the store opened at 9am, and the crowd broke through the safety barriers.

Three people were killed in a crush outside a soccer stadium ahead of a World Cup qualifying match. The incident occurred as fans rushed to buy tickets and surged while waiting to enter the 35,000-seat stadium through its four entrances. The victims were trampled, and all injured individuals were taken to the hospital.
At the end of a soccer game lights went off due to a power outage causing fan to run for the exits. Another 8 people were injured, 3 critically. The stadium was recently built.
Passengers rushing to catch a leaving train struggled on stairs during one of the biggest holiday of the year. It is believed two women slipped on the stairs triggering the accident.

People got killed at the gates as they tried to surge into the president's palace when the gates were thrown open. That day a political march was organized, with the number of participants (hundreds of thousand) underestimated. Some reports suggest people were hoping for banknotes handout from the president.

A pilgrim at a religious festival attracting more than 300,000 people fell on the steep and narrow stairs to the temple, which had become slippery with palm juice used in the offerings, and caused a crowd incident. Subsequently, an electric wire short-circuited at a nearby store, causing a fire that spread to cooking gas cylinders, which then exploded.

An accident occurred on the more shaded area as stadium gates for a film festival were opened in a scorching day. That day also featured a concert by a well known singer. Between 10 and 20 people were injured.

Two children died and 45 others were injured in a accident at a soccer match. The accident occurred when hundreds of fans tried to force their way into the 20,000-seat stadium after officials attempted to close the gates. The match continued despite the chaos.
An accident occurred after the game when 100,000 spectators moved to the exits. About 40 others were hurt, 5 of them in critical condition. No reason was given for the rush to the doors.
An accident occurred during a bathing religious festival. It is believed some people slipped while walking on the path connecting two holy pools where pilgrims were dipping. Dozens were injured.
A teenager was trampled upon by fans who were finding their way into a stadium to watch Kenya play Morocco in a World Cup qualifier.

One person in a crowd of about a million pilgrims on their way to a temple shouted that another pilgrim was carrying explosives. Fearful of suicide bombers, the crowd surged towards a closed bridge. Despite the gates at both ends of the bridge being locked, the crowd forced their way in but were unable to escape from the far end, causing deadly overcrowding. Many people also fell off the bridge and drowned.

A bomb hoax resulted in a sudden evacuation aboard a plane which was about to take off. 62 passengers were wounded in the accident. Emergency exits were opened to let passenger evacuate the plane. 424 passengers and 19 crewmembers were on board.

At least 11 people were killed and more injured in an accident before a concert, when about 5,000 people rushed to enter a gate at an outdoor stadium, causing a chain reaction of collapses. The victims included mostly people in their 60s, as well as a seven-year-old and two teenagers. Police suspect the rush was driven by a desire for better seats.

Several accidends of various extent took place several times over two days as thousands of people gathered at the residence of an industrialist to collect gifts. Details remains unclear, although it is believed another 50 were injured.

As students were about to leave after evening classes a blackout occurred and someone shouted "ghosts", getting everyone rushing to the staircase where the accident occurred. Another 27 have been injured, some seriously.
An accident occurred as a man was distributing cash in a car park. As voices started to circulate more and more people rushed causing a collective motion killing 7 and injuring 40.
People began to gather very early for a food relief supply campaign organized in a school. As soon as the gates were opened people stormed in leading to the accident. Dozens were injured.
At least 22 students were feared killed in an early morning crowd accident caused by a fire at a carpenter's workshop behind the school. The tragedy occurred as students rushed to escape the blaze.

At 3am a crowd of about 4,500 people began to gather at a distribution center offering relief supplies (open was scheduled for 9am). Shortly afterward it began to rain heavily and rumors spread that distribution of supplies would be limited to 1,000 families. At around 4am, the crowd broke through a police cordon and surged into the entrance of the building. People at the front of the crowd slipped and fell on the sloping ground of the entranceway. There were enough supplies for 45,000 families.

A tragic accident occurred outside a nightclub, where three young girls were killed and several others injured due to overcrowding. The nightclub's owner  was found guilty of causing general danger by failing to ensure safety, and was sentenced to five years in prison.

Abandoned luggage caused an incident among pilgrims attending the Stoning of the Devil ceremony. Pilgrims got killed as they tripped over luggage left at the entrance of a bridge and were overrun by the crowd behind.
The excited crowd rushed the stage at a concert, killing three and injuring 42.

Around 30,000 people gathered outside a stadium for a public event. Games with cash and luxury goods as prizes were scheduled to start at 6am, with tickets only available on the day at the venue. Once the festival began, the organizers announced that only the first 300 people could participate in the prize games. This lead to a huge crowd surge. The security hurried to close the gates, but the crowd behind did not stop, and the gate collapsed, causing the accident.

During the elections significant disorganization and long delays led to chaos and large crowds forming outside many polling stations. In one such incident, a 75-year-old man died at a voting center. The long wait in the heat, combined with frustration, caused tension among voters, leading to some forcing their way into the buildings.

An accident occurred on stairs during a 10,000 packed religious event attended by women. Reports are unclear, but they agree that a women stopped on stairs to assist a child, leading some to stumble and ultimately leading to a collective motion toward the only narrow exit located below the stairs. Over 100 people suffered injuries.

A crowd incident occurred as participants left a stadium that was used as a venue for a presidential election rally. It is believed people in the overcrowded stadium rushed toward the stage to get a glimpse of the president, leading to the collapse of an iron fence. More than 200 were injured.
An accident occurred as doors of a temple were opened and thousands of devotees tried to enter. Around 50,000 pilgrims were waiting for hours to enter the shrine. All dead were over 70 years old.

Aften an evening class, middle school students swarmed into the staircase of a three-story building killing 6 and injuring 39. It is unclear what triggered the collective motion, with a witness suggesting someone was seen squatting to tie shoelaces on the stairs as hundreds rushed downstairs.

During a religious festival around 9,000 nearly naked men gathered inside a temple to compete for sacred wooden sticks, believed to bring good fortune. At around midnight, the lights were turned off and the sticks were thrown into the crowd from above. This triggered an intense, chaotic scramble, with participants pushing, surging, and piling on top of each other in a confined space.
A major surge occurred during an open air concert as enthusiastic spectators saw stars appearing on stage. Several people fell down eventually resulting in the fatalities.
Spectators leaving a soccer game rushed for the free shuttle buses, causing a crowd incident. Another 40 people were injured.

A brick wall collapsed as victims were trying to reach a free mosquito net handout in a community hall. Some people tried to reach the location by climbing the walls causing the collapse. 6 more people were injured.

As three overcrowded trains carrying thousands of pilgrims to a religious event arrived at a station, a platform change was announced. A crowd incident occurred as the passengers tried to make their transfers. 14 women died and more than 40 were injured.
A crowd incident occurred as 150,000 spectators were leaving a stadium after watching a public execution. Another 34 were injured. Details remain unclear.

An incident took place on a bridge leading to a hilltop temple during a religios event used on both directions. It is reported that thousands of people crowded a narrow path leading to the temple. Some pilgrims are thought to have been pushed off the bridge while others fell over.

A supermarket offered a 20% discount on cooking oil. As the store opened, a large number of customers who had been waiting outside were admitted at the same time, causing a crowd incident. 3 people were killed and more than 30 injured.

An evacuation at an university, caused by an electrical fire, left at least three people dead and more than 100 injured. The fire broke out in a crowded lecture hall, and although it did not spread, it led to a flee, and some students jumped from second-floor windows.

An accident occurred as thousands of pilgrims attempted to garland a statue in a temple, killing 6 and injuring another 15. More than 100,000 pilgrims gathered at the hill-top temple which had only one gate used for both entry and exit.

An accident in a railway station resulted in the death of a passenger, who was injured during a chaotic rush as stranded passengers tried to board trains after days of waiting. The station was crowded with 260,000 passengers, part of a larger backlog of 1.4 million people trying to leave ahead of the Spring Festival.

A deadly accident occurred after a concert as people tried to get in at the same time when hundreds attempted to get out through the only gate. Unconfirmed reports suggest the venue was filled over its capacity of 700.
A high-density crowd of 100,000 pilgrim gathered at a temple for a religious event. Crowd pressure caused a fence to collapse and many of the pilgrims fell over, causing a crowd incident.

A crowd accident resulted in the deaths of five people, and left over 40 injured. The incident occurred during an inaugural function when devotees, scrambling for water in the intense heat, created a chaos. The injured were hospitalized, and authorities noted that the lack of proper facilities contributed to the tragedy.

An accident occurred as gates were closed, leaving thousands of people wanting to enter the full stadium outside. It is believed that many fake tickets were in circulation, leading the stadium to get filled as people still tried to enter. All the casualties occurred in front of a single gate.

Police raided a nightclub filled with 500 young people for drugs and underage drinking, causing the guests to flee. The only emergency exit was locked, and a crowd incident occurred, killing 12 people.

An accident occurred in front of a temple's gate as the deity was taken out. A crowd surge occurred which caused those accompanying the deity to fell down. 12 were injured. It is said one million people congregated for the 9-days festival.

An accident in a football stadium during a graduation ceremony for youth entering national service resulted in 23 deaths, and left 36 people injured. The accident occurred as the crowd was attempting to enter the stadium, resulting in the fatal crush due to poor organization and overcrowding. The ceremony continued afterward.

A rain shelter collapsed at a temple where 3,000 followers had gathered for a religious event. Someone who saw the collapse shouted that there had been a landslide, and those in the crowd who heard this rushed towards the perimeter fencing that had been installed by the police, causing a crowd incident in which the fence collapsed and 146 people died. It was raining at the time.
Riots started between fans after a player conducted a "witchcraft" to weaken the opposite team. Crowd escaping the riot resulted in an accident, killing 11 and injuring others.

A crowd accident killed 21 women and injured dozens more as a crowd rushed to receive a cash handout from a philanthropist. The incident occurred when around 2,000 people pushed through a narrow alley. The victims, weakened by fasting and the intense heat, were trampled in the crush, with some suffering from oxygen shortages. Police have criticized the poor organization of the event.

An accident happened at the moment the door of a temple was opened resulting in the destruction of the barricades. A slope leading to the temple resulted in many people to loose footing. Rumors have blamed a bomb blast nearby and possibly also a collapsing wall could have caused the collective reaction. More than 425 people were injured.
Children were killed by a possible combination of lack of oxygen and crowd pressure in an overfilled disco hall. Occupancy was reported being twice the recommended number.

A crowd of over 2,000 people gathered at a store on the day before a bargain sale. As the opening time of the store approached, the crowd smashed the glass doors at the store’s entrance. Five minutes before the store was scheduled to open, a store employee got killed under the collective crowd motion. The store was due to open at 5am and the crowd had begun to gather at about 9pm on the previous day, filling the available space by 3:30am.

An accident at a disco killed four people when news spread of free entry. About 200 people rushed to the venue, and the crush left one person hospitalized and seven others treated at the scene. Quick actions by police helped prevent further casualties.
An accident occurred during a sales promotion where giveaways were given to the people. Two people were killed and 11 were injured in the accident.

A crowd of fans without tickets had gathered outside the stadium, and rushed the entrance just before the start of the match. The entranceway collapsed due to the sudden crowd pressure, causing an incident. Over 100 people were injured.

An incident occurred as spectators attempted to leave in a hurry a 70,000 packed free concert leading to the collapse of a wire fence. The event was moved to a larger venue to meet a growing demand. Delays in the concert schedule may have contributed to the sudden move and some blamed police decision to close some gate.

Two people were killed during a large gathering at the banks of the Ganges to watch the solar eclipse. One person drowned, while the other was crushed in an accident caused by the heavy rush of pilgrims, with an estimated 60,000-70,000 people present.

A rumor started among a group of students who had got wet in the rain on their way to school that rain leakage had caused a short circuit and they were at risk of electrocution. In the ensuing chaos, a counterflow occurred on the stairs as students tried to flee, causing a crowd incident. There were five deaths and 33 injuries.

An accident occurred during a charity flour distribution killing 20 and injuring more than 30. The incident occurred in an overcrowded area when hundreds of women, desperate for aid during Ramadan, rushed into a small charity office. The chaos was exacerbated by a power outage and a security guard’s use of force to manage the crowd.

52 classes ended simultaneously meaning that between 2,000 and 2,500 students left their classrooms at the same time. The crowd became concentrated on the single staircase close to the student dormitories, causing a crowd incident. It was raining heavily outside. Eight people were killed and 26 were injured.

A disaster occurred as gates of a temple were opened during a cerimony which could accommodate 50,000 people despite the estimated 100,000 present. It is believed somebody slipped triggering the disaster.

An accident occurred at a jetty as people surged to board a boat during a religious festival. It is believed only a bamboo barricate stood betweeen the crowd and the jetty which easily crumbled. Dozens were injured.
An accident occurred at the entrance of a mosque as people had to squeeze through a narrow road because the main road was closed for construction. Another 55 people had been injured.

A rumor claiming someone had been electrocuted by faulty electric wiring spread among a crowd of 10,000 people that had gathered at a temple distributing free food and clothes. The crowd all tried to flee at once, rushing through a gate that was still under construction, and the gate collapsed. Subsequent crowds tried to get through, leading to a crowd incident. Many of the victims got killled under the collapsed gate.

Around 30,000 to 40,000 aspirants showed up in a recruitment center to apply for a police job. In total 100,000 forms were given out although 3,600 positions were available. The accident occurred as the gates were opened. 11 people were hurt.

A 9-year-old girl died from head injuries sustained in a school accident, which occurred when hundreds of students, mobilized to clean the campus after heavy snow, rushed to a storeroom, causing a collapse when one student stumbled. Three other students were injured but are in stable condition.

A person died while queueing for World Cup tickets as thousands of people waited overnight. A seizure while in the line was identified as the apparent cause of death. Ticket sale system had been recently changed from internet to ticketing offices to allow a wider access to the public.

Conflicting reports exist to explain a sudden surge which occurred during a religious function. Some believe a women mistook a thick rope for a snake and started screaming, others believe the surge was caused by a sprinkling of holy water. A structural collapse was also blamed by some. A squall hit the venue during the function.
A last minute platform change triggered a collective motion during summer rush with many people carrying luggages. 15 people were injured.

An accident during the annual Rath Yatra festival killed a woman and left four others seriously injured. The incident occurred when the chariot began moving, and a large crowd surged towards it. The deceased was trampled in the chaos, despite efforts by the police to control the crowd. This was the third such stampede at the festival in recent years.

An accident occurred when a crowd of people trying to get to a concert venue encountered a crowd that was trying to leave on a connecting road with a shared entrance and exit. More than 500 people were injured. The access road to get to the venue had a gentle uphill slope.

An accident at a disco hall killed two young girls and injured 13 others. Children between the ages of six and 17 had gathered. While most of the injured were treated and discharged, three children with serious injuries remained in the hospital.

An argument over sacrifying goats during a religious festival triggered a simultaneous motion of the 40,000 people who had gathered at the temple. It is believed a commotion occurred as people tried to get their goats sacrified first. Another 11 people were injured.

An accident occurred as fans were trying to force their way into a stadium when a gate collapsed. It is believed the group did not have the tickets and did not want to pay, although other reports point out that gates were already closed despite the stadium being not filled. At the time it was raining heavily.

4 million people attended a festival held on an island. The island and the mainland both became overcrowded. A crowd of about 7,000 people became trapped on the bridge to the island by the crowds on both sides and were unable to move for several hours. Rumors spread through the crowd that the bridge would collapse and that they were at risk of electrocution, causing widespread chaos. 347 people were killed and 755 injured.

An accident at a bar during New Year celebrations killed 10 people, mostly young adults aged 18 to 25. The incident occurred shortly after midnight, possibly triggered by party-goers trying to flee a fight.

According to several sources, an out-of-control jeep collided with pigrims coming back home in an unlighted steep narrow forest stretch. The event triggered pirgrims to flee, ultimately leading to 106 deaths and injuring more than 450. However, details remains mostly unclear.

An accident without an apparent trigger occurred in an overcrowded nightclub killing 3 and injuring 14. It is reported that about 2,500 were in the nightclub, designed for 307, while 4,000 tickets had been printed. A witness said he could not touch the ground for five to ten minutes due to extreme crowd density.

People were leaving a packed stadium after a presidential rally. A police officer appearently shot in the air in an attempt to control the crowd, but resulting in a surge. Main gate was said to be locked forcing people to use narrow passageways. Some report that people were trying to leave while others attempted to get in at the same time.

An accident occurred as people were leaving a stadium where a religious cerimony had been held. The crowd surged against a metal barrier, killing 36 and injuring 64, most of them women. Some reports suggest the stadium was filled over capacity.

About 5,000 supporters of a political leader gathered for a valedictory party. Party leadership asked supporters to queue and started distributing gifts. After hours in line, when it started to get dark, believing the presents may be insufficient, people started jumping the queue resulting in an accident killing 25.
An accident whose details are rather unclear happened at the entrance of a stadium where a music festival was held. It is said a large crowd gathered, with people outside outnumbering people inside.

At least eight people were killed and 58 injured in an evacutaion on a ferry, following an explosion. Fear erupted among the 875 passengers after the blast. This incident was the fourth sea transport disaster in a week in Indonesia.

An appearent announcement about couches leaving had people rush toward the exit in a nightclub (coach travel was included in ticket cost). Some reports suggest a fire alarm was also triggered, worsening the situation. It is unclear whether capacity was exceeded or not.

An accident occurred as people were trying to enter a sacred place during a religious festival for which around 200,000 gathered. It is believed the accident occurred as pilgrims were climbing stairs to cross a bridge leading to the venue. Crowd management was said to be handled by volunteers. 50 pilgrims were injured.

Thousands of fans gathered outside a sold-out stadium in the hope to get tickets on the spot. When a gate was momentarily opened fans rushed to get in leaving 2 people dead. In the days preceding the match disorders occurred as the tickets sold out.

After a concert in a private college, private security guards used batons to push back a group of fans who rushed toward a singer to get his autograph. This led to a second rush toward the exits where the accident occurred. The main gate was closed although details on the reasons are unclear. Venue was said to be over capacity.

A woman was killed in a dense crowd as thousands of students attempted to seek precious remaining slots from a university. The accident occurred as the gates were opened. 3 people were critically injured. Most of the people were poor students who were not able to apply online and had to apply on-site.

Pigrims attempted to pass through the closed gate of an area hosting the fire oven on which the faithful walk. An accident occurred killing 12 and injuring four. Police attempt to force back the crowd was blamed by some, while police suspect some may have succombed due to the severe cold.

Violent home supporters stormed into the field leading the opposite team and their fans to run into their side to shelter from the attack. An accident occurred during the escape as stadium gate was closed. Many fatalities were caused by knife injuries or direct violence, but appearently also improper crowd management had an impact. More than 500 were injured.
A collective rush appearently occurred after brakes of a four-wheeler failed at a fair venue. Tens of thousands of devotees had gathered for the fair concluding a religious event.

During the funeral of a religious leader attented by ten thousands of people, 3 participants got killed and another 137 were injured. The accident happened at the cathedral square. A non-lethal stempede was also reported as gates were opened, before church officials scrambled to close the doors again.

A crowd accident resulted in the deaths of nine people and left six others critically injured. The incident occurred when over 5,000 people gathered for a chance to be hired by a company, but impatience set in as they waited for the gates to open. When the crowd overpowered the security guard and stormed the premises, the crush ensued.

An accident at a hastily organized event at an under-construction sports complex, resulted in the death of a 65-year-old and left over 200 people injured. The chaos occurred when the crowd, some of whom had been waiting since early morning, rushed into the venue after the gates were opened, with no proper crowd control or facilities. The event, which lacked adequate planning and preparation, was marred by severe overcrowding, dehydration, and heatstroke. Confusion was caused by the last-minute change in the venue.

It is believed that time constraints to perform an "auspicious sight" led to an accident in which 3 people died. A dozens sustained serious injuries. Some claims the accident occurred as devotees were passing through the exit and police was absent. Police claims the deaths were caused by hearth attack after climbing the stairs.

An accident occurred inside the hall of a religious institution possibly after an elderly women slipped and fell, leading others to fall over in overcrowded conditions with no space to move. Crowd control was performed by temple volunteer inside the institution with police staying outside. 30 were injured.

An accident occurred late at night after devotees had been waiting in line for the whole night. Some indicate a person fainted leading to the accident, others blame the use of force by the police. The hot weather and high humidity possibly had a contribution, with some also reporting only one out of five exits were used.

An accident occurred in an exit corridor of an arena during a large music party. Venue was found to be over capacity (which was not clearly defined since music events had been never hold in the sport facility) and not long before the accident a cargo gate (not intended for pedestrian access) was opened letting many people in. Crowd control was missing or nearly missing within the premises.

A power outage in the darkness sparked a rush among devotees close to a river to get to the main road resulting in an accident where at least 17 people died. Some claims electricity was cut after a report of a naked electricity wire. It is reported that police control room received the information of the accident more than 1.5 hours later from a journalist.

An accident occurred as people were going back home after a firework display. The incident occurred as people exited the stadium and according to some witnesses two large crowds moving in opposite directions collided causing the tragedy. It is believed around 50,000 were on the streets. 200 were injured.

Several people have been killed while trying to enter an overcrowded stadium for a church vigil. Accident occurred at the gates as many more than the 70,000 expected people turned up. According to reports all gates were used and the tragedy occurred as people were moving in through a partially slippery surface.

An accident took place at the railway station where millions of pilgrims were converging to take a holy dip for the Kumbh Mela religious festival. Reports are conflicting on the cause, some suggesting a railing collapsed others blaming police use of force to control the crowd.

Students from a primary school attempted to leave the dorm to take morning class but the gate was not open as usual, blocking the flow and causing the accident. When the gate was opened it was already bent by the pressure of the crowd.

An accident occurred as worshippers attempted to get holy water distributed for free for a special occasion. The number of people attending was underestimated with police and army unable to control the crowd attempting to enter the church.
People escaping a riot between spectators during a boxing match scrambled out of the stadium killing 18 and injuring more than 40. The facility accodating 1,500 the day of the match had 2 exits.

A crowd accident occurred during a rally, leading to several injuries and up to four fatalities. The incident happened when a crowd, unable to enter due to locked doors, tried to force their way in when one door was briefly opened. People were trampled while attempting to gain access to the stadium. The large turnout exceeded expectations.

An accident occurred at a temple resulting in the death of a 60-year-old man and injuries to 17 others. The incident happened when a large crowd, which had been waiting for hours to enter the temple, became impatient after the gates were closed. When one of the gates was opened to allow an ambulance to leave, people rushed in, causing the rush.

Many pilgrims were killed on a 7-m wide and 400-m long bridge when a 25,000 estimated crowd was over it. Rumors saying the bridge was about to collapse resulted in a collective rush as some people drawned after jumping from the bridge. Sources are however conflicting on what caused the collective rush.
An accident occurred when a political leader was giving out gifts and money to his supporters. Similar accidents already occurred in the same place under similar cirumnstances.

An accident occurred during a vigil in a church. It is believed more than 50,000 people (some suggesting 100,000) gathered for the event while the church could accommodate only 5,000. It is unclear what caused the accident, some relating it to a commotion, others to people shouting "fire".

An accident occurred outside a mosque as a crowd rushed to take offerings of a traditional food. Possibly organizers were not sufficiently prepared for the high number which showed up as the event was held on a Sunday.

An accident occurred in a fair as people were watching a dance in a theater. The accident was possibly caused by a clash between two groups or rumours of fire. Lighting was reportedly insufficient at the venue.
Supporters assembled in front of the residence of a spiritual leader to mourn his death. An accident occurred as gates were opened and people burst in. 18 people died and 56 were injured.

As part of a recruitment program 100,000 applicants tried to enter a stadium with a capacity of 60,000 where tests were scheduled. Gates remained closed by security men and an accident occurred as anxious job-seekers tried to jump fences to enter the venue. The resulting chaotic situation killed 10 and injured more than 40. Nationwide 6.5 million people had paid an application fee to apply for 4,000 positions.

An estimated 28,000 job-seekers tried to enter the stadium with a capacity of no more than 15,000. They had all already paid an application fee to take part to the tests scheduled on the venue. The crowd started to run for safety after soldiers started shooting in the air in an attempt to contol the crowd. 3 of the victims were pregnant women, more than 20 were injured.

A school was used to perform tests for a nationwide job application. The venue was successfully filled with 11,000 candidates by 7am. However, a two kilometers long line formed in front of the venue. Fearing the situation would escalate personnel resorted to tear gas, causing 7 people to collapse and trampling 3 to death. Space around the venue was limited due to the large number of vehicles parked there by applicants.

About 25,000 applicants were invited for a job interview in a stadium accomodating 16,000. Several job-seekers arrived early in the morning but were not allowed into the main bowl and some gates were locked. As one gate was opened people stormed in leading people at the front to collapse. Some also report that rifles were fired in the air in an attempt to control the crowd.

Power generators were cut out during a tribute concert to a recently deceased singer, plunging the stadium into darkness. An accident occurred as people tried to leave the venue, killing more than 20.

During an important soccer match fans began throwing stones to the pitch to which police responded with teargas, eventually leading to the crowd fleeing toward one exit where the accident occurred. Another 10 people were injured. It is believed the stadium was packed beyond capacity.

Hundreds of people leaving a concert on a beach troubled while rushing through the single exit. The accident happened at the end of the show, which was attended by up to 10,000 people. Dozens were injured.

Thousands (reportedly 50,0000) of devotees gathered to celebrate a religious event. During the procession a live electric wire shocked a woman and set off chaos, triggering an accident in which people broke through rope barriers. 10 pilgrims were killed and another 12 were injured.
Nine people were killed in an accident outside a military academy when relatives tried to visit cadets, violating visitor regulations. Five others were injured and hospitalized.

Six pupils were killed and 26 others injured in a crowd accident at a primary school. The accident occurred when a cotton cushion in the dormitory building blocked the staircase, causing students to trip and triggering a rush of others down the narrow stairs. The local government has ordered a city-wide safety inspection of all schools.

Tens of thousands of people gathered to watch a religious event on a lawn ground surrounded by buildings. An accident occurred as people were rushing to leave after the event, some blaming a rumor of a live high tension wire falling on the ground. Around 15 people were injured.

An accident occurred at a political rally as people rushed together towards the main gate as a speech ended. Circumnstances remain unclear, with some blaming that only two of the five gates were opened and other reporting lights were switced off. Over 100 people were injured.

An estimated crowd of 30,000 leaving a stadium from a single exit after a religious service caused an accident killing 11 and injuring 40. Since only one exit was open, some tried to break off parts of the wall to leave, prompting the police to fire teargas thus causing a rush in the crowd.

A crowd of 300,000 gathered to assist to New Year celebrations. An accident occurred on a stairway leading to a viewing platform as people were moving on both directions. Reasons for the rush on both directions are unclear, some blaming rumors of a show cancellation, other reporting cash coupons were trown on the crowd.

Police used tear gas to attempt dispersing crowds of about 6,000 ticketless fans who were making their way into the stadium for a soccer game. After three years of restrictions on games' attencance, 5,000 tickets were up for public sale, with another 5,000 distributed through the club. Fans were known for their history of battling the police.

An accident occurred during the carnival parade as a man onboard of a float hit an overhead power line creating a spark which triggered a sudden rush within the surrounding crowd. The man survived the shock but 18 from the bystanders were killed and another 78 injured.

An accident occurred on the riverbanks as pilgrims were moving through narrow passages to take holy dips. The annual festival usually attracts one million people, but on this edition the number was much higher since it came on a public holiday. Some reports that rumors emerged about a collapsing bridge, although main cause is not clear.

Metal fences collapsed killing several fans during a concert in an underground club. It is believed a group of people tried to enter the venue by force despite the premises were already at full capacity, although others blame the use of force by guards with whom a clash took place.

An accident occurred after a large crowd gathered for clothes that were being donated. More than 1,500 people had assembled outside the clothing factory and a collective rush occurred as people tried to force their way through a small gate. More than 50 people were injured.

An accident occurred at the riverbank where devotees were taking a holy dip. The accident occurred at a narrow entrance to the banks as people leaving the river clashed with people trying to bath in a particularly auspicios time. The festival is held once every 144 years and 40 milion pilgrims were expected over 12 days. 15,000 policemen and 171 cameras were used to monitor the situation.

An accident occurred around 5am as some of the 150,000 pilgrims who gatheted for the religious festival surged towards the entrance of a temple after doors were opened. People sleeping in the kilometers-long queue were overran as others pushed toward the entrance. Around 50 were injured.

An accident occurred at a T-intersection between two streets leading to a bridge during the annual Hajj pilgrimage. Exact dynamics of the incident is unclear and also the number of victims is disputed. That year temperatures were extremely high and political tensions existed between countries from which most of pilgrims arrived.

An accident occurred during a party organized in a university. Due to a lack of control at the entrance, organizers were not able to keep the number of attendee under control, reaching a total of 1,500. The tragedy occurred at the connection between two buildings as people tried to move in opposite direction under high crowd density.

An accident occurred during a religious event held in a stadium at 1am. A report claims that when people were leaving the venue from the single exit others started to get in, while another version claims the accident was caused by a robbery attempt.

Students of a high school got killed as they rushed to escape the school building after chaos broke out following a strong earthquake. More than 30 were injured. The school was not damaged by the quake.

Four people drowned following a collective rush in a temple pond. Causes remains unclear but officials sources admitted they failed to estimate the actual number of pilgrims and were not able to keep the crowd under control as rituals started in the early morning. The accident came as the deity was taken toward the pond.

A crowd accident occurred at a Ramadan celebration resulting in the deaths of nine people and left several others injured. The incident occurred at a community center, with conflicting reports about its cause—some witnesses claim a power outage triggered the collective motion, while others say the doors were locked, preventing people from exiting.

An accident occurred after police used tear gas during a protest at a religious festival. Rubber bullets and baton charge were also used. It is believed some threw stones to the police. An estimated two million people gathered for the event in a period during which several protests started in the country.

An accident occurred on stairs as people were moving out during a political rally through the only gate (although some reports suggest two were employed). It is reported that the accident occurred under intense heat. According to some sources 100,000 people attended the rally.

An accident occurred as people heading to a religious event tried to cross a bridge at once. 4,000 people were expected but 50,000 showed up. The surging crowds killed one man and rumors that the bridge could collapse fueled more chaos.

An accident occurred as people were leaving a stadium where a music festival was being held. It is reported 15,000 tickets were sold for a venue with a capacity of 8,000. Some reports claim doors had been locked and police was looking for criminals inside the venue. Another 40 were injured.

A crowd of worshippers struggled to pass through the doorway of a synagogue to attend the healing service of a famous prophet. In the process three people collapsed and died. The accident occurred at 4:30am when thousands of people already filled the venue.

A collective rush broke out while people were waiting to board a boat from an island where a religious event was being held. Other sources attributed the fatalities to hearth attack. A large crowd was waiting for a boat as many urged to come back as they would have had to wait 8 hours for the high tide to dip again. At least 1.5 million people took their holy dip over two days.

An accident at a railway station during a promotion event led to the death of one person. The chaotic scene occurred when thousands of fans rushed to the train after a popular actor waved at them, prompting a lathicharge by police to control the crowd. Several others were injured, and two police officers also fell unconscious due to the suffocation.

An accident occurred as hundreds of supporters stormed the stadium gates. The crowd pushed against barriers after failing to gain entry to the venue before the match. It is believed the stadium would have not be sufficient to accomodate the crowd. At least 60 were injured.

Competition over food aid resulted in an accident as thousands of poor people struggled to claim food handouts. It is reported 35,000 people jostled to enter the stadium where the distributuon was organized. 28 others were injured.

Two people were were killed when spectators rushed to the stage during a open air concert. The show was attented by 350,000 people, although the event was planned to handle half of that number. Dozens were injured.

An accident occurred in a primary school when students gathered in the bathroom in a short period of time. The incident happened during a 10 minutes break while preparing for monthly examinations. Some reports suggest the wall of the bathroom collapsed. More than 20 were injured.

A crowd accident resulted in the death of a woman and injuries to at least two others, when thousands of devotees gathered in a spiritual place to perform rituals. Around 20 lakh people attended the event, and when barricades were removed, the crowd surged, causing the tragic incident.

Hundreds of fans (allegedly in possess of a ticket) tried to break past stadium's barricates during an important match. Some reports claim the stadium was oversold, possibly due to fake tickets being sold. Police fired tear gas and used water cannons in an attempt to control the crowd. Another 25 were injured.

An accident occurred during a public viewing in a square attended by thousands as some people shot pepper spray in an attempt to steal valuables. This caused a rush in the crow, which eventually led to the collapse of a railing leading to an underground garage. More than 1,600 people were injured, many because of the broken bottles on the ground.
A tragic incident occurred in which five people were killed and about 14 others injured during a scramble for charity at the residence of philanthropist. Details remain unclear.

An accident occurred at a free soccer game in a stadium. Gates' opening time was delayed by about three hours and when the gates finally opened the accumulated crowd rushed in the stadium, killing 8 and injuring around 40, mostly children. Some report the gates were force opened and police fired tear gas.

Around the end of an important match, a wall collapsed under crowd pressure after fans tried to escape from violent supporters throwing projectiles between them. Police fired tear gas at clashing supporters in an attempt to disperse them causing what could have exacerbated the situation. More than 40 were injured.

An accident occurred as fans attempted to pour through a gate for a well anticipated derby. The situation went under control when all gates were opened. It is believed the sales of fake ticket at entry gates contributed to the accident. The stadium had a capacity for 87,000 spectators and was sold out.

An accident occurred in a refugee camp hosting tens of thousands as clothings were thrown from relief trucks. It is reported the distribution was not authorized by the agencies managing the refugee camp and similar accidents already happened as private distributions were improvised on the camp.

An accident occurred on the stairs of a tiny overbridge connecting platforms of a train station as four trains arrived simultaneously during rush hour. At the time it was raining and possibly someone slipped on the already packed stairs which were used also as shelter from the heavy rain. Another 30 were injured.
