An accident occurred at a narrow lane which was used by pilgrims to reach and leave a river where they were taking a holy dip during a religious event. All victims were elderly women. The event was attended by thousands of devotees. Another 10 were injured.

An accident occurred during annual food aid distribution at a local market. It is reported a crowd of 800 (larger than usual) was attracted in the town with 8,000 inhabitants. Another 40 were injured. Details on the accident remains unclear, although some report that barriers collapsed.

An accident occurred at the end of the funeral service of a political leader when ritual food was arranged in a venue. The accident occurred as gates of the community center were opened and people rushed despite the announcements that enough food was available to everyone. It is reported around 8,000 people took part to the event.

An accident occurred during an army recruitment drive after the gate of the ground was opened at 3am. Applicants were asked to wait in line but when the gate opened people began jostling to enter the venue. An estimated 4,000 aspirants had been waiting in cold weather. Another 4 were injured.

An accident in a stadium following a religious ceremony resulted in six deaths, including five women. The incident occurred when the crowd, exiting the event, rushed toward a small entrance instead of using two larger ones.

An accident occurred at a presidential inauguration held in a stadium. Thousands of people had been waiting in queue from the morning, but president's supporters tried to force their way through a gate for cars of invited guests. This sparked police response which resulted in the accident. 90 were injured.

Over 20,000 people had assembled for gift distribution (foor and money) in front of a mosque. When the gate was opened at 9am some fell and got overrun. Many have been waiting the whole night. It is reported the ground could hold 3,500 people at most and security was very limited. Another 50 were injured.

Following a fight during a student's party attended by 500, some individuals threw a tear gas canister. Partecipants attempted to evacuate the venue, but the only narrow exit was located at the bottom of a staircase and was closed, leading to an increase in crowd pressure killing 19. Reasons for the door being close remain unclear.

A crowd surge at a hall entrance occurred as public was allowed to pay homage to the remains of a deceased political leader. Police were letting in people in batches but a rush occurred and they were not able to control the crowd, resorting to force to push back the crowd. More than 20 were injured.

Supporters started to form kilometers-long queues from the early morning for an important match scheduled at 2:30pm. The accident occurred as some supporters wanted to get in despite the gate being closed because the stadium was full. At least 37 people were injured.

Soccer fans were killed as they flooded out a stadium after a game. The accident happened as supporters found the gates shut after the match. When the police finally opened the gates, people rushed out with some falling down and ultimately killing 5 and injuring another 7.

An accident at a railway station resulted in the deaths of two people and left 14 others injured, including two children and two women. The incident occurred when passengers rushed to board two trains arriving simultaneously, causing overcrowding on the foot overbridge.

An accident occurred during a performance at a festival. Despite announcements to control the crowd, attendees became frantic and rushed toward the stage, breaking barricades and throwing chairs. The situation escalated into a crush, resulting in the death of one person and several others being injured. Police had to intervene to restore order.

Some individuals sprayed an "irritant substance" inside a nightclub prompting the occupants to flee the venue. However, only one emergency exit was open and a metal railing on the way out collapsed, causing people to fall off the walkway. About 1,400 were in venue with a capacity of about 470 (it is believed some few hundreds sneaked into the venue). 200 people were injured.

People from a religious community assembled at the show ground of a church to attend a service by a famous preacher. When heavy downpour started, people push each other in a scramble to find shelter. The accident left 3 dead and another 9 injured.

More than 1,000 people showed up at an indoor market for free food coupons given out to elderly people, but participants were not aware that only 200 were available. Two people (78 and 85 years old) passed away as they were waiting in the pushy crowd. Only 4 people at a time were allowed to get into the office and register for the coupons.

An accident occurred during an afternoon rally as thousands of supporters tried to follow the president's convoy out of the stadium through a partially locked gate. People were seen rushing and pushing to each other to leave the stadium. Around 15 people died and dozens were injured (sources vary on the exact number).

An accident took place in a nightclub when the gate was opened to get a crowd of more than 600 in the venue accomodating a maximum of 500. Shortly afterwards a forward rush occurred and the whole queue collapsed, killing 3 people and injuring 4. It is said 100 people collapsed to the ground after the surge occurred.

An accident during a temple ceremony killed seven devotees, including four women, and injured ten others. The incident occurred when a large crowd surged forward to collect temple coins distributed by the priest. The event, which draws many devotees seeking prosperity, lacked proper crowd control and security measures, contributing to the tragedy.

An accident occurred at a stadium during the independence day. After the military parade finished, gates were open to let people in for a free concert by a famous singer. However, gates were suddenly shut afterwards. Believing entry was possible the crowds kept moving toward the closed gates, killing 16 and injuring about 80.

An accident occurred at a festival taking place every 40 years, where around 1.7 lakh devotees had gathered to pay respects to the deity. Four people were killed. Some officials criticized the inadequate crowd management, while others attributed the incident to the unexpectedly high turnout.

An accident in a temple led to one death and eight injuries. The chaos occurred as crowds of devotees attempted to enter the temple, breaking through police barricades. The deceased, whose body showed no signs of injury, likely died from cardiac arrest. The situation was quickly brought under control, and the injured were taken to the hospital.

An estimated 30,000 people gathered at a stadium for the concert of a famous singer. The accident occurred as fans thronged through the entrance. The concert was scheduled for 7pm, but people started gathering from mid-afternoon. It is said there were only four entrances allowing people to enter one at a time and the stadium could accomodate only 15,000.
A wall collapse near a temple, triggered a collective motion, resulting in four fatalities and 21 injuries. Details of the accident remain unclear.

Pilgrims gatherered for a traditional run of 2-3 km through the built environment. During the run, attended by hundreds of thousands, an accident happened, probably because a walkway collapsed or somebody trippled, leading others to fell over him. 31 people were killed and more than 100 were injured.

An accident at a campaign rally resulted in at least 10 deaths and 98 injuries. The chaos occurred when a large crowd tried to exit the stadium through a single gate after the rally, causing people to be trampled. Six women and four men were killed, and many others were hospitalized, with 14 in serious condition.

People climbing over entry barriers during a free concert organized in a park and attended by around 8,000 people caused the barriers to give way ultimately leading to a collective rush killing at least 3 and injuring more than 30. It is said that queue had formed from the early morning with the concert scheduled for 10am.

Armed criminals escaping from the police entered a street party as they continued firing. The 5,000 people who had gathered for the party fleed in search for a shelter, as police closed the street and threw pepper spray canisters. The rush for shelter resulted in 9 people getting killed and injuring around 20.

A tragedy occurred during the burial of a political leader as millions gathered for the final funeral event in a series of ceremonies. The streets were overcrowded, and eyewitnesses reported that road closures contributed to the chaos.

At least 20 people were killed and 16 injured in an accident during a church service. The incident occurred when a large crowd surged forward to touch holy oil poured by a popular preacher, in hopes of being healed. Witnesses described the chaos as people trampled each other in a desperate rush for the anointing.

A crwod accident killed at least 14 pupils and injured nearly 40 others, some critically. The incident occurred as children were leaving school in the afternoon, with some reports suggesting that a narrow staircase collapsed during the rush.

An accident left at least 23 people dead as refugees rushed for food and clothing at a community center. The area is home to nearly 250,000 displaced individuals, many of whom fled terror attacks and other violence in neighboring countries.

An accident occurred during a charity event aimed at distributing money to local residents on the eve of Ramadan. Around 300-400 people gathered for the donation, but chaos and mismanagement led to the deaths of three women and left nine others injured, four of whom were critically hurt.

At least 13 people dead and three others were injured in a nightclub as partygoers tried to escape a police raid. The raid targeted the illegal gathering of over 120 people, violating COVID-19 restrictions, including a curfew and social distancing measures. As people fled, they were crushed on the stairs.

A crowd accident killed at least 15 people, including 11 women. The crowd had gathered at a football stadium to collect visa application tokens. Despite heavy police presence, jostling broke out among those waiting. The consulate had resumed issuing visas after a seven-month pause due to COVID-19.

An accident occurred during a Christmas package distribution by a Non-Governmental Organization. At least two people were confirmed dead, and several others were injured as hundreds rushed to receive the packages. The chaos led to people being trampled in the rush.

A crowd surge occurred near the ticket counter during a musical event as attendees rushed in when the gates opened. As a result two women died and four were injured. Investionagion to focused on whether the event had the necessary permissions.

An accident occurred in a stadium leading to the death of 45 people. Thousands had gathered to mourn the deceased president, and overcrowding combined with attempts to force entry into the stadium reportedly contributed to the deadly incident.

An accident occurred during an annual pilgrimage when attendees slipped on a wet metal slope leading to a staircase, causing a deadly pile-up. Prior warnings about overcrowding risks had been issued, and COVID-19 precautions may have unintentionally created dangerous choke-points.

Amidst the chaotic evacuation efforts following the change in the political leadership of the country, several tragic incidents occurred involving large crowds attempting to flee the country. In one of those accidents seven civilians lost their lives in the crowds near the airport including some children.

A tragic crowd surge occurred during a musical event, resulting in the death of ten people and numerous injuries, primarily due to compressive asphyxiation. Despite prior safety concerns and visible distress signals, the concert continued amid overwhelming logistical failures, such as an inadequate crowd-to-staff ratio and poor planning for surges.

An accident occurred during a religious crusade in a stadium. The incident resulted in the deaths of two children and injuries to several others. The accident was reportedly related to overcrowding and poor crowd management as attendees attempted to exit the venue simultaneously.

An incident took place in the early hours, around 2:30 AM, near the gate of a shrine atop a hill resulting in the deaths of 12 pilgrims and injuries to 16 others. The crowded roads leading to the temple became packed, with witnesses describing a surge when a large crowd left the temple, causing people to fall and get trampled.

An accident occurred during an open-air church gathering resulting in the deaths of at least 29 people, including children. The accident was reportedly in relation to a group of armed men, wielding knives and machetes, who attempted to rob the attendees, causing the crowd to flee.
An accident occurred at the entrance of a stadium during a soccer game resulting in at least eight fatalities and 38 injuries. The incident occurred as fans were attempting to enter the arena.

A disaster occurred during a student assembly convened to discuss upcoming elections for the local university federation. During the gathering, an individual detonated a tear gas canister. The ensuing chaos led to a flee as students desperately attempted to evacuate the confined space.

An incident took place during a charity event aimed to distribute food and gifts to attendees. Due to the anticipation of large crowds, the venue was shifted from the church premises to a polo club. However, as early as the night before, thousands had gathered, and by the morning of the event, the crowd's impatience led to a surge towards the gate.

An incident occurred in a stadium during a soccer match. Despite only 15,000 tickets being issued for safety reasons, the stadium was packed with an estimated 45,000 supporters, including many without tickets. As the match approached, two victims were caught in the overcrowded and chaotic entry process, likely asphyxiated and trampled.

An incident occurred at the country's border, resulting in the deaths of at least 23 migrants and injuries to many others. Approximately 2,000 migrants attempted to cross the border fence. The situation escalated into a violent confrontation with security forces, leading to a chaotic situation resulting in uncontrolled crowd motion.

An incident took place in a temple district during a monthly fair when a large crowd had gathered for "a glimpse of the deity." As the temple doors opened at 5 am, the surging crowd led to a sudden motion resulting in the deaths of three devotees and injuries to several others.

An accident occurred during a concert resulting in nine fatalities and approximately 20 injuries. The incident took place as thousands of attendees attempted to exit the venue through limited access points, leading to overcrowding and chaos.

Chaos ensued during a soccer game when supporters stormed the pitch after their team's loss. In response, police fired tear gas, leading to a crowd crush as fans attempted to flee the stadium. The accident resulted in 135 deaths and 583 injuries.

An accident occurred in a narrow steep alley in an entertainment district known for its vibrant nightlife. An estimated 100,000 people had gathered to celebrate Halloween, marking the first major event since the easing of COVID-19 restrictions. The dense crowding in the confined space led to a deadly surge, with many individuals suffering from compressive asphyxiation.

An accident occurred during a concert in a stadium. The stadium, which has a capacity of 80,000, was reportedly overfilled, with some estimates suggesting attendance exceeded 120,000 people. This overcrowding led to a crush that resulted in the deaths of 11 individuals, including two police officers.

An incident occurred in a concert venue as a large number of people without tickets attempted to force their way into the venue, leading to overcrowding and a subsequent crowd crush. Eight individuals were hospitalized, with four in critical condition immediately following the event.

An accident occurred during a political rally resulting in the deaths of at least eight people and left several others injured. The incident occurred as people gathered to see a politician and a rush to catch a glimpse of him caused a cement railing to collapse, leading to people falling into a drainage canal.

An accident occurred during a gift distribution program by a politician, resulting in the deaths of three women and injuries to several others. The accident occurred after a barricade collapsed, causing chaos among the 4,000 women gathered for the gifts. Another accident occurred only few days before with a similar dynamics.

An incident took place during a New Year's Eve celebration when attendees moved to a narrow corridor to watch fireworks, leading to overcrowding and a deadly crush. The accident resulted in multiple fatalities and injuries.

An accident occurred as hundreds of desperate citizens rushed to obtain subsidised wheat flour distributed by the government. The chaotic scramble resulted in the death of a labourer, and several others sustained injuries.

An incident occurred during a fair resulting in the death of one woman and injuries to over 20 others. The incident occurred on a T-juction as a large crowd, including women and children, gathered to visit a temple and attend the fair after a two-year gap due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

An accident occurred in a stadium during a soccer game. Thousands of fans, many without tickets, had gathered since dawn in hopes of attending the match, leading to overcrowding and chaos. The situation escalated leading to the accident which resulted in multiple fatalities and injuries.

An accident occurred during a concert in a nightclub. As attendees were exiting the venue around 11:00 p.m., a sudden rush toward the exits ensued, reportedly triggered by unfounded fears of gunfire. This ensuing chaos led to a crowd surge resulting in three fatalities and several injuries.

An accident occurred during the distribution of free flour on the first day of Ramadan. The event led to a chaotic situation resulting in the death of one person and injuries to eight others. Similar accident followed in a few days.

An accident occurred in a stadium during a free flour distribution event. The chaos was triggered by a technical issue with the verification app, which caused long delays and frustration among the over 1,500 women present. The situation worsened when police, instead of calming the crowd, used force, including baton-charging.

An elderly died after being injured in an accident at a free flour distribution center. The incident occurred as a large crowd rushed to get flour, and the victim was pushed, causing him to fall and sustain critical injuries. Allegations surfaced that the disorder was linked to the poor arrangements and complaints about eligibility for free flour.

Another accident occurred at a food distribution center during a charity drive organized by a private company resulting in multiple fatalities and injuries. A large crowd of about 400 women had gathered to receive rations, and in the absence of proper crowd control measures and without prior notification to local authorities, the situation escalated.

A crowd accident occurred during a charity event where hundreds had gathered at a school to receive financial aid before the end of the Ramadan. Shots were fired into the air in an attempt to control the crowd, accidentally hitting an electrical wire, which caused it to explode, creating the chaos that led to the crowd collapse

A crowd crush occurred in a stadium during a football match as fans attempted to enter the stadium. Authorities have suggested that factors such as the over-sale of tickets and the circulation of counterfeit tickets may have contributed to the overcrowding and subsequent tragedy.

An accident occurred in a stadium during a football match shortly before kickoff when a surge of fans attempted to enter the stadium and individuals at the entrance forced their way in, leading to a collision with those already inside. This caused many to be trampled or asphyxiated. Some were trapped against railings, and others fainted.

An accident occurred in a stadium during an opening ceremony resulting in at least 12 fatalities and approximately 80 injuries. The accident happened as a large crowd of about 50,000 spectators attempted to enter the stadium, leading to a deadly crush.

An accident occurred in a stadium during celebrations for a special holiday, resulting in at least six fatalities and approximately 100 injuries. The incident took place at dawn as thousands of attendees attempted to enter the stadium for the annual public holiday event.

A crowd accident occurred during a religious event where over a hundred devotees had assembled for the event. As soon as the gate was opened, the crowd rushed in, leading to the accident. The district administration responded promptly, rescuing the victims and transporting the unconscious women to a hospital.

An incident occurred in a railway station resulting in the death of at least one person and injuries to several others. The incident took place amid heavy overcrowding as passengers rushed to board a train for a festival. Authorities attributed the accident to an unexpectedly large number of passengers, causing chaos on the platform.

An accident occured during a military recruitment drive organized in a stadium. The campaign, which aimed to enlist 1,500 young people, attracted a large crowd, leading to chaos as applicants, frustrated by long waits, attempted to force their way into the stadium. The accident occurred late at night, with many individuals being trampled in the rush.

An accident occurred in a university musical festival when a sudden downpour prompted a large crowd to rush for cover, leading to the collective escape. The event happened during a concluding festival, with students already waiting outside an auditorium for a concert.

Two women lost their lives during New Year's Eve celebrations taking place in a bar and attended by approximately 2,000 people. The suburb area was congested with both the crowd and haphazardly parked vehicles, creating a chaotic environment. Police efforts to control the situation were hampered by poor lighting conditions.

An accident occurred during a rice distribution event. The distribution was initiated the previous day, aiming to alleviate economic hardship by selling bags of rice at a subsidized rate. The event attracted a large crowd, leading to a chaotic situation where seven individuals lost their lives.

An accident occurred in a university during the distribution of rice palliatives intended to alleviate hardship. The incident resulted in the deaths of at least three students and injuries to 14 others. Overcrowding and inadequate crowd control during the distribution event led to chaos, precipitating the situation.

An incident took place during an almsgiving event organized by a private company and resulting in the deaths of seven individuals. The accident happened around 10:00 am as a large crowd gathered to receive charitable donations.

An accident occurred during a religious event attracting approximately 250,000 attendees, significantly exceeding the permitted capacity of 80,000. The severe overcrowding led to an accident as attendees attempted to exit the venue, resulting in the deaths of many women and children.

An accident occurred during a gospel concert in a stadium resulting in the deaths of at least nine people, including two women, and leaving several others injured, some critically. The victims were appearently caught in overcrowding as the crowd surged during the event, although organizers claim attendance was well below the venue's capacity.

An accident occurred in a temple resulting in the deaths of seven pilgrims and injuries to nine others. The victims included five women and two men. The incident, reportedly triggered by a fight between a flower seller and devotees, escalated when security forces attempted to control the crowd with batons, leading to chaos.

A jailbreak attempt in overcrowded prison led to at least 129 deaths, mostly caused by a stampede as inmates tried to escape. Gunfire broke out during the chaos, with 24 inmates shot and dozens crushed. The prison, built for 1,500 but holding over 12,000 people, became a scene of chaos and violence.

An accident occurred in a stadium during a football match, triggered by a controversial refereeing decision in the 82nd minute, which led to unrest among supporters, who invaded the pitch and threw stones. In response, security forces used tear gas to disperse the crowd, causing chaos and an crush as spectators tried to escape the stadium.

During a Christmas funfair in a school an accident occurred, killing several attendees. The event promised cash gifts, food, and scholarships to children, drawing a crowd of over 5,000 people. As attendees gathered hours before the start time, the overwhelming turnout led to a deadly crush when the organizers arrived to begin the distribution.

An accident during a charity event for food aid distribution led to at least three deaths. The crush occurred before the event officially began as a large crowd rushed forward, primarily impacting women and children. The event was canceled, and the church held a marriage ceremony after the crowd was evacuated.

Early in the morning at a church, an accident during a Christmas food distribution drive led to ten deaths, including four children, and left eight others injured. Over 1,000 people, many waiting since the previous night, surged forward around 6:30 a.m., causing chaotic scenes as people fell and were trampled.
